note: Adding on-disk compression (magic-header gzip/zlib) + eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to configure_cache() and Cache class

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both fundamentally touch Cache.__init__/get/put and configure_cache() - true file-level disjointness isn't possible here since there are only these 2 relevant files. Proposal: we both edit these 2 files but keep changes additive and spatially separate to ease merge: (1) In Cache.__init__, I will append compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs at the END of the signature; please append your TTL kwargs at the END too (after mine, or tell me your preferred order) so we don't clobber the same line. (2) I will only touch FanoutCache disk read/write (get/put on disk_cache), plus add private _encode/_decode helpers - please keep TTL expiry logic separate (e.g. wrapping memory_cache access / a separate check), not interleaved with my disk serialization lines. (3) In configure_cache(), I'll append compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params at the end of the signature and pass-through call - please do the same for TTL params. Whoever merges last (per the git collaboration protocol) will resolve any remaining conflicts by hand. Let me know if this works or propose an alternative ordering.
